title: 国力电子：电真空器件在可控核聚变装置中的核心应用 (Guoli Electronics vacuum-electronic devices for controlled nuclear fusion)
authors_or_org: Guoli Electronics (国力电子 / Kunshan Guoli Electronics Technology Co., Ltd.)
claim_supported: Guoli manufactures hydrogen thyratrons, high-power klystrons, and vacuum capacitors positioned as core components in China's controlled-fusion devices, bridging macro power systems and plasma control.
notes: Establishes Guoli as an active domestic vacuum-capacitor supplier into an adjacent CN big-science channel beyond semiconductor fabs.

institutions.name: Kunshan Guoli Electronics Technology Co., Ltd.

title: Hydrogen case study: operational efficiency and electrolyser technology at the Sinopec Kuqa project
claim_supported: Kuqa produced 2,010 tonnes green hydrogen in H2 2023 (~20% of rated capacity); Q4 2025 target is 20,000 tonnes/year at 51% capacity; documented issues are insufficient power supply and electrolyser underperformance at low load, with LCOH risk of up to 20% increase.
notes: Primary-adjacent CN demand-anchor source for F-23's Kuqa utilization/degradation-audit timing trigger.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.

title: Problems at world's largest existing green hydrogen project will not be solved until late 2025, Sinopec admits
claim_supported: Published 2024-01-03. Kuqa's 260MW electrolysers are supplied by three Chinese manufacturers -- Cockerill Jingli, LONGi, and Peric -- all alkaline technology; shared design flaw prevents safe operation below ~50% of maximum output, holding the facility to ~20% capacity in its first six months.
notes: Central finding for F-23: confirms Kuqa and its named suppliers (LONGi, Peric) are alkaline, not PEM -- a technology mismatch with the idea's PEM-specific product framing.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: FuelCellChina is a China-focused hydrogen/fuel-cell industry information portal. Not India.

title: Peric Hydrogen 2026, 6.5 GW Capacity with LONGi (Top Chinese Electrolyzer Manufacturers)
claim_supported: PERIC's 6.5GW and LONGi's 3.5GW manufacturing capacity are alkaline (ALK)-only; Sungrow (3.0GW) covers both ALK and PEM; alkaline electrolysis accounts for 36.7GW of China's total manufacturing capacity, described as a commoditized industrial product.
notes: Confirms both of F-23's named CN design-in-channel partners (LONGi, Peric) are alkaline-technology manufacturers, reinforcing the S02 technology-mismatch finding.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: Industry-analysis publisher; no India registration or affiliation found. Content concerns CN manufacturers, verified independently against S02.

claim_supported: Published 2025-10-11. Hesuan Zhineng (合锻智能, 603011.SH) won a RMB209M tender for BEST (Compact Fusion Energy Experimental Device) vacuum-chamber segments 1-4, targeting delivery by end-June 2026, with welding roundness <=1mm and machining precision +/-0.05mm ("ITER同级标准"); first batch of gravity supports delivered May 2025.

title: 中国的大对撞机，何时能成？(China's giant collider -- when will it happen?)
authors_or_org: 中国新闻周刊 (China Newsweek), via Sina Finance
claim_supported: Published 2025-12-08 (China Newsweek issue #1215). Per IHEP researcher Wang Yifang, CEPC was not recommended by CAS for inclusion in China's 15th Five-Year Plan; construction (if approved) could start ~2027 and complete ~2035; CEPC's fate depends on CERN's ~2028 decision on the competing FCC project -- if FCC is approved, CEPC becomes unnecessary; if rejected, CEPC could reapply for the 2030-2035 planning cycle. First-phase CEPC cost estimated at RMB360B, ~70-80% China-funded.
notes: Negative timing update to G-01's originally named CEPC trigger -- directly informs the window verdict and the rationale for the binding multi-tender-cadence condition.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.

claim_supported: Published 2025-04-08. ISO 11137-1:2025 increased allowable sterilization energy levels from 10 to 11 MeV for electron beams and from 5 to 7.5 MeV for X-rays.
notes: Confirms and dates the permissive-ISO-change binding condition as accurately described by the idea; the associated 2028 named-uprater evidence gate remains unmet.

title: Telix Completes Acquisition of ARTMS, and its Advanced Isotope Production Platform
claim_supported: Published 2024-04-11. Telix Pharmaceuticals completed acquisition of ARTMS (Vancouver, Canada) for US$57.5M upfront (equity + cash) plus up to US$24.5M in contingent earn-outs and royalties; ARTMS becomes a stand-alone technology arm within Telix's vertically-integrated supply chain.
notes: Central merchant-targetry-incumbency finding: the idea's presumed independent cyclotron-target supplier (ARTMS) is now captured by a vertically-integrated radiopharma company, reinforcing the binding re-scope-away recommendation for the cyclotron-target leg.
